command:
python -u -c 
import urllib.request, ssl, os

output_dir = 'tasks/t0002_literature_survey_dsgc_compartmental_models/assets/paper/10.1016_j.neuron.2016.04.041/files'
os.makedirs(output_dir, exist_ok=True)
out_path = os.path.join(output_dir, 'sethu_2016_ach-gaba.pdf')

# Try the Cell.com pdf URL with browser-like headers
url = 'https://www.cell.com/neuron/pdf/S0896-6273(16)30155-6.pdf'

ctx = ssl.create_default_context()
ctx.check_hostname = False
ctx.verify_mode = ssl.CERT_NONE

headers = {
    'User-Agent': 'Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/120.0.0.0 Safari/537.36',
    'Accept': 'text/html,application/xhtml+xml,application/xml;q=0.9,application/pdf,*/*',
    'Accept-Language': 'en-US,en;q=0.9',
    'Referer': 'https://www.cell.com/neuron/fulltext/S0896-6273(16)30155-6',
}

try:
    req = urllib.request.Request(url, headers=headers)
    with urllib.request.urlopen(req, context=ctx, timeout=30) as resp:
        data = resp.read()
        ct = resp.headers.get('Content-Type', '')
        print(f'Status: {resp.status}')
        print(f'Content-Type: {ct}')
        print(f'Size: {len(data)} bytes')
        print(f'First bytes: {data[:8]}')
        if data[:4] == b'%PDF' and len(data) > 10000:
            with open(out_path, 'wb') as f:
                f.write(data)
            print(f'SUCCESS: Saved to {out_path}')
        else:
            print(f'Not a valid PDF')
except Exception as e:
    print(f'Error: {e}')
